chapters.label: CHAPTER 01
chapters.title: Patient or Machine
chapters.summary: A treatment room contradiction opens the case.
chapters.anchor: A patient lies on a table in a hospital treatment room.

chapters.label: CHAPTER 02
chapters.title: The Narrow Margin
chapters.summary: Radiation therapy depends on mode and hardware matching.
chapters.anchor: Radiation therapy works by aiming a beam of high-energy particles at a tumor.

chapters.label: CHAPTER 03
chapters.title: The Safety Net Removed
chapters.summary: Proven code inherits trust from hardware that no longer exists.
chapters.anchor: The Therac-25 was not a new machine.

chapters.label: CHAPTER 04
chapters.title: The Hidden State
chapters.summary: Fast edits expose a software state the console cannot admit.
chapters.anchor: The Therac-25's software had a flaw that only appeared under specific conditions.

chapters.label: CHAPTER 05
chapters.title: How Trust Repeats
chapters.summary: Routine sessions train the system to believe the machine.
chapters.anchor: Here is how a machine earns trust it does not deserve.

chapters.label: CHAPTER 06
chapters.title: The Pattern Repeats
chapters.summary: Separate hospitals see the same impossible injury.

chapters.label: CHAPTER 07
chapters.title: The Burden of Proof
chapters.summary: The case breaks open when testing asks how the machine can fail.
chapters.anchor: The investigation that finally exposed the full scope

chapters.label: CHAPTER 08
chapters.title: Trust Is Not Safety
chapters.summary: The lesson moves from one machine to any system that grades itself.
chapters.anchor: The Therac-25 is now one of the most cited case studies
